name: Point West
description_short: Point West Credit Union is a Portland-based, member-owned financial institution serving 90+ years with a focus on financial inclusion for immigrants, communities of color, and small businesses.
description_long: Point West Credit Union has operated for more than 90 years in the Portland metropolitan area as a not-for-profit, member-owned financial institution. Originally known as Hacienda CDC Credit Union, it has evolved into a community-focused credit union dedicated to serving underserved populations. In 2016, Point West earned designation as a Juntos Avanzamos credit union—a national recognition for credit unions committed to serving and empowering Hispanic and immigrant communities—making it the first such designated credit union in Oregon. The organization emphasizes financial inclusion and community empowerment as core values. Point West reports that 40% of its membership consists of people of color, reflecting its commitment to serving diverse communities. The credit union operates multiple branches in the Portland metro area and provides digital banking access through mobile and online platforms. Point West accepts applications from individuals regardless of immigration status, explicitly supporting members on their citizenship journeys. The institution positions itself as fundamentally different from traditional banks, emphasizing relationship-based banking and community support. Point West offers a comprehensive suite of financial products including personal and business checking and savings accounts, personal and business credit cards and loans, HELOCs, vehicle loans, consolidation loans, and its signature Boost Loan product—a payday loan alternative designed to serve members needing quick access to funds. The credit union also provides tax filing assistance services to help members navigate tax season affordably. Point West distinguishes itself through intentional community focus, member testimonials spanning decades of membership, and specific product design for underserved populations. The Boost Loan represents a deliberate alternative to predatory payday lending, featuring fair rates and flexible terms. The institution's long operational history and Juntos Avanzamos designation signal established legitimacy and commitment. However, as a credit union, Point West's membership eligibility requirements and geographic limitations to the Portland metro area may restrict access for some consumers. Current advertised rates (4.99% APR on new vehicle loans, 6.00% on HELOCs, 10.24% on consolidation loans) position competitively but require credit qualification. Limited information is publicly available regarding membership eligibility criteria, minimum deposit requirements, or specific Boost Loan terms on their website.
services: Business loans and lines of credit
pros: 90+ year operating history as established not-for-profit credit union with local Portland roots
Juntos Avanzamos designated credit union explicitly serving Hispanic and immigrant communities without citizenship requirement
40% of membership consists of people of color, reflecting genuine diverse community focus
Boost Loan product offers payday alternative with fair rates and flexible terms instead of predatory fees
Member-owned cooperative structure with emphasis on relationship banking and community support
Competitive advertised rates: 4.99% APR new vehicle loans, 6.00% HELOC, 10.24% consolidation loans
Multi-channel access via mobile app, online banking, physical branches, and CO-OP ATM network
cons: Geographic limitation to Portland metropolitan area restricts access for consumers outside service territory
Website lacks specific details on membership eligibility criteria and minimum deposit requirements
Limited public information about Boost Loan APR, fees, and maximum loan amounts on website
Credit qualification required for most products; rates shown are promotional/best-case scenarios
Fewer physical branch locations compared to major national banks
best_for: Hispanic and immigrant communities seeking banking without citizenship documentation barriers
Small business owners and entrepreneurs in the Portland metro area
Underserved populations prioritizing relationship-based banking over corporate institutions
Consumers needing payday loan alternatives with fair terms and community accountability
diagnosis: Point West Credit Union is best for Portland-area residents, particularly Hispanic and immigrant communities, small business owners, and underserved populations seeking relationship-based banking with explicit community commitment. The primary caveat is geographic limitation to the Portland metro service area and the requirement to meet credit qualification standards for most products offered.
typical_results_timeline: Membership approval and account opening typically takes 1-3 business days. Loan decisions are usually faster than traditional banks.
meta_description: Point West Credit Union is a Portland-based credit union serving 90+ years with focus on financial inclusion.
